name: Almond Praline Bell
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F. Grease a 9", 10-cup decorative mold or use a 10-cup bowl. Also grease a large cookie sheet. Toast almonds on another cookie sheet until golden brown (about 20 minutes). Grind almonds in a blender or food processor to make 1 1/2 cups.
2. In a heavy, 3-quart saucepan, combine granulated sugar, light-brown sugar, honey, and cold water. Cook, stirring with a wooden spoon, until the sugar is dissolved.
3. Continue cooking, uncovered, without stirring, until the mixture reaches 300°F on a candy thermometer or until a few drops of syrup form hard, brittle threads when dropped into cold water.
4. Remove from heat; stir in butter and ground almonds.
5. Pour into prepared cookie sheet. Using the lemon halves, work quickly to smooth out the praline and push it from the center to the edge of the cookie sheet until it is 1/8 inch thick.
6. Lift the praline off the sheet before it is completely cooled and hardened, and mold it inside the prepared decorative mold.
7. Allow to cool completely (about 2 hours) before unmolding.
8. Unmold just before serving by carefully loosening the candy with a spatula and removing it from the pan.
Makes 1 large bell.
Note: Leftover pieces can be kept in a closed jar at room temperature or wrapped in aluminum foil.
